summary: Re-tested the MetricLiteral expansion fix on GET /api/metrics/summary and related endpoints. CONFIRMED: regression closed. Created /app/backend/tests/test_healthbridge_v7_metric_literal_regression.py with 37 focused pytest cases — ALL 37 PASS. Additionally re-ran the prior iteration_4 Phase C2 suite (test_healthbridge_v6_phase_c2.py) — now 22/22 green (up from 21/22; the previously-failing test_metrics_summary now passes). Verified: (1) /api/metrics/summary returns HTTP 200 with all 33 metrics matching DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE — steps, heart_rate, sleep, workouts, spo2, ecg, calories, stand, distance, active_minutes, floors, workout_count, vo2_max, training_load, recovery_time, calorie_intake, protein, carbs, fat, water, fiber, weight, bmi, body_fat, muscle_mass, sleep_quality, resting_hr, hrv, blood_pressure_sys, blood_pressure_dia, respiratory_rate, body_temp, stress — and each item has the expected schema (metric/label/unit/current/goal/trend). (2) /api/metrics/summary/all returns 200 with the same 33 metrics. (3) /api/metrics/categories returns 200 with 5 categories (activity, exercise, nutrition, body, vitals) and total_metrics=33; flattened metric ids exactly equal the 33 expected. (4) /api/metrics/{metric}/detail for ALL 25 newly-added Literals (distance, active_minutes, floors, workout_count, vo2_max, training_load, recovery_time, calorie_intake, protein, carbs, fat, water, fiber, weight, bmi, body_fat, muscle_mass, sleep_quality, resting_hr, hrv, blood_pressure_sys, blood_pressure_dia, respiratory_rate, body_temp, stress) returns 200 — NO 422 path-param validation errors. (5) Regression smoke: /api/connectors (>=9), /api/metrics/availability, /api/auth/me all still 200. No new regressions detected.
critical_code_review_comments: MetricLiteral (server.py:63-75) now correctly enumerates all 33 metric names matching DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE — fix is solid. Consider extracting a single MetricId enum derived from DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE keys (or computed at module init) to keep the two lists in lock-step automatically and prevent recurrence the next time a metric is added.
server.py is now 2019 lines — still well above the 700-line threshold flagged in iteration_4. Recommend splitting into routers/ (connectors, devices, metrics, watches, ai_insights, billing, admin).

success_rate.backend: 100% (37/37 v7 regression + 22/22 v6 Phase C2 re-run)

test_credentials: admin@healthbridge.app / ySk4rWp4nSn5KsB8WvI4iF (verified working via /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Per-test fresh users use phc2_<uuid>@example.com / Pass1234! pattern (carried over from v6 suite).
seed_data_creation: None new. v7 suite is read-only (login + GET endpoints only); does not mutate any state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL=https://b5756d38-6d5c-4694-ae0a-e29e724ded9b.preview.emergentagent.com, API=/api. Iteration 4's sole HIGH bug (Pydantic ResponseValidationError on /api/metrics/summary) is RESOLVED. MetricLiteral at server.py:63 now lists all 33 metric names. Phase C v2 suite 22/22 green. New regression suite test_healthbridge_v7_metric_literal_regression.py is read-only and safe to re-run; suggested as a permanent smoke for any future MetricLiteral / DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE additions. Still-open code-quality item: server.py is 2019 lines and should be split into routers/.
rca_of_issue: Original bug (iteration_4): MetricLiteral on the /metrics/summary response model was a stale Literal of only 8 metric names while DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE produced 33 — FastAPI's serialize_response raised ResponseValidationError on every item whose metric name fell outside the 8. Fix applied by E1: expand MetricLiteral (server.py:63-75) to enumerate all 33 metric ids grouped into ACTIVITY/EXERCISE/NUTRITION/BODY/VITALS comments. Verification (this iteration): GET /api/metrics/summary now returns HTTP 200 with 33 items, /api/metrics/summary/all returns 33, /api/metrics/categories returns 5 categories totalling 33, and /api/metrics/{metric}/detail accepts all 25 newly-added Literal values without 422. No new regressions in the v6 Phase C2 suite (22/22).
